faqs.q: What does augmentation mean in psychiatry?
faqs.a: Augmentation means adding a second medication to strengthen the response to a first one that's giving only partial relief. The first drug stays in place while a second is layered on top.

faqs.q: Does augmentation mean my first medication failed?
faqs.a: No. A partial response is still a response, and augmentation treats it as a foundation worth building on rather than a dead end.

faqs.q: Can I boost my medication on my own with supplements?
faqs.a: Augmentation is a prescriber's call made with knowledge of your full medication list and history. Adding supplements or other medications on your own can carry real risks, so it's best discussed with a clinician.
